design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore non-linear levels, solve environmental puzzles, defeat alien creatures, access terminals for objectives and plot exposition, manage shield/oxygen resources, locate save points
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (shields, oxygen) combined with spatial complexity and enemy encounters; infrequent save points create high-stakes progression
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Security officer defending a massive colony ship (the Marathon) against alien invasion; detective/explorer uncovering conspiracy through terminal narratives
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rare for its era in prioritizing narrative complexity as fundamental to gameplay—plot is gated behind terminal access and required for level progression. Rejects frequent checkpointing in favor of precious save points. Emphasizes exploration and environmental storytelling over combat; many levels require objective completion beyond enemy elimination

difficulty.continues_system: Death reverts player to last activated pattern buffer save point; infrequent placement throughout levels
difficulty.checkpointing: Manual save via pattern buffer devices placed sparingly throughout levels; some levels contain no save points, forcing single-run completion
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal; difficulty appears fixed per level. Player can only manage risk through resource awareness (shield/oxygen monitoring) and tactical positioning

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon and equipment pickups; shield/oxygen recharge stations provide resource management layer rather than traditional progression upgrades
progression.unlock_mechanism: Sequential level access through terminal activation and objective completion; specific tasks must be accomplished before teleportation to next level (item retrieval, switch activation, enemy elimination, NPC protection)
replayability_hooks: Secret terminals containing easter eggs and designer messages
Non-linear level layouts encourage exploration for alternative routes and hidden content
Multiplayer deathmatch mode supports up to 8 players on LAN
Multiple objective types per level (reach endpoint, retrieve items, clear enemies, protect allies)

world_building_techniques: Computer terminals as primary narrative delivery—AIs provide objectives and plot exposition
Environmental storytelling through level design (e.g., hazard-filled corridors, alien-infested areas)
Diegetic documents accessible via terminals (engineering logs, crew diaries, AI conversations)
Ship architecture and setting (Mars moon Deimos-constructed colony ship) establishes sci-fi context
Enemy placement and area conditions reveal story progression (alien invasion stages)

faction_indicators: Human crew members (indicated by green squares on motion sensor)
Alien invaders (indicated by red triangles on motion sensor)
Ship's artificial intelligences (Marathon, Durandal, Leela) as authoritative voices guiding player
danger_signals: Motion sensor activity (red triangles indicate enemy presence)
Low shield/oxygen indicators warning of imminent death
Hazardous environmental conditions (molten pits, crushing ceilings, magnetic fields, oxygen-free zones)
Locked doors and inaccessible areas signaling incomplete objectives
environmental_storytelling: Areas populated by human NPCs indicate safe zones or mission-critical locations
Enemy-infested sections reveal alien invasion progress and combat necessity
Obstacle-heavy corridors (narrow passages, crushing ceilings, hazard pits) communicate danger and puzzle intent
Low-gravity and oxygen-free environments signal special level mechanics and environmental hazards
